questions.text: I often initiate change rather than wait for conditions to force it.
I tend to exhaust my own resources before asking others to contribute theirs.
I tend to quietly handle background work that makes everyone else's contribution possible.
I prefer straightforward conversations where everything is on the table.
I find that I gravitate toward the choice that most benefits my personal situation.
I experience new places, people, and situations with a heightened sense of emotional arousal.
I believe that the moral concern generated by witnessing hardship is a resource that should be channeled, not suppressed.
I would rather be the founder than the early employee.
I experience the onset of emotion as a sudden physical sensation before I can even name what I'm feeling.
I find that I naturally notice when someone is being strategically vague.
I hold the view that treating suffering as a genuine moral priority — in every decision, every day — is the standard to which I hold myself.
I let my energy and interest guide what I work on at any given moment.
I am highly aware of who is making eye contact with whom in a group.
I am content to contribute to something larger without needing personal recognition.
I review my goals regularly and adjust my plans to stay on track.
I create backup plans because I know that primary plans sometimes fail.
I hold the view that objective evaluation of distressing circumstances is a form of intellectual honesty that serves everyone better than emotional reaction.
I tend to take unexpected news in stride without a pronounced emotional spike.
